text: 0.7g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, the whole appeal is cheap, filling nutritional payoff.
63.4g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
21.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet.
magnesium at 32% daily value, iron at 25% daily value, potassium at 27% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first: filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. does a lot of jobs at once because protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

on the weaker side: protein quality still has a ceiling because good, but not meat or dairy level. can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier because dry legumes especially ask more from the cook.
S tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
